steps.title: Trace the structure
steps.content: The text first characterizes moral philosophy through examples, then explains Hart's point that some works give a method for moral living without a motive. The final sentence contrasts those with works that give both method and reasons.

steps.title: Reject invented structures
steps.content: Choice B invents a claim about popularity and impossibility. Choice C treats the passage as a history plus research proposal. Choice D invents a comparison of which work is more widely read. The answer is A.
